entity.summary: Incorporate a private company limited by shares with ACRA — a S$315 set-up (S$15 name application plus S$300 incorporation) that gives shareholders limited liability from day one. Based on the founder's track record building and exiting a Jakarta food-delivery chain, the founder(s) also qualify for an EntrePass on the innovator track alongside the standard incorporation.
entity.callout: EntrePass-eligible via entrepreneurial track record — no need to wait for a Singapore hire to sponsor a pass.
nextSteps: Reserve the company name and file the S$315 ACRA incorporation.
Prepare the EntrePass innovator-track application citing the Jakarta track record.

visaCompass.summary: With 5 relocating PMETs (2 founders, 3 staff) — well under the 25-PMET small-firm threshold — Warung Digital's Employment Pass applications get the neutral default score on both C3 (Diversity) and C4 (Support for local employment): 10 points each instead of being assessed on actual hiring mix. Combined with an assumed qualifying salary (C1: 20) and a recognised degree (C2: 10), the illustrative total is 50 points, comfortably above the 40-point pass threshold.
visaCompass.callout: A brand-new small firm defaults to a neutral COMPASS score on diversity and local-hiring criteria — it does not need to already have a diverse or majority-local team to pass.
nextSteps: Confirm the actual offered salary and applicant qualifications before filing — C1/C2 here assume a qualifying benchmark, not a real candidate.
Track headcount growth against the 25-PMET threshold, since the neutral scoring stops applying once it's crossed.

licenses.summary: F&B retail requires a Food Shop Licence from the Singapore Food Agency before opening — S$195 per year — and it must be applied for before starting food-retail operations.
licenses.callout: Required, not optional: apply for the Food Shop Licence before the first day of trading.
nextSteps: Apply for the SFA Food Shop Licence ahead of the planned opening date.
Budget S$195/year as a recurring licensing cost.

taxIncentives.summary: Singapore taxes chargeable income at a flat 17% corporate rate, with 75% of the first S$100,000 and 50% of the next S$100,000 exempt for qualifying companies in their first three years of assessment. GST is 9% on standard-rated supplies, but registration is only compulsory once taxable turnover exceeds S$1 million — at a projected S$450,000 in Singapore revenue, Warung Digital is well under that threshold for now.
nextSteps: Claim the start-up tax exemption for the first three years of assessment.
Re-check the S$1 million GST registration threshold as revenue grows.

banking.summary: As a foreign-owned company, expect enhanced due diligence and a longer review when opening a business bank account — DBS requires ACRA incorporation documents, signatory identity/address verification, and board resolutions up front.
nextSteps: Prepare ACRA documents and signatory ID/address verification before the first bank meeting.
Start the account-opening process early to absorb the longer foreign-owned review window.

timeline.summary: A roughly 4-week runway: reserve the name and incorporate in week 1, apply for the Food Shop Licence in week 2, open the corporate bank account in week 3 (padded for foreign-owned due diligence), and submit both the EntrePass and any staff Employment Pass applications in week 4.

riskMatrix.summary: The two live risks are a foreign-owned-company bank account delay (medium likelihood, medium impact — mitigated by preparing documents early) and a lower-likelihood, medium-impact flag: once headcount crosses 25 PMETs, the neutral COMPASS scoring on C3/C4 no longer applies, so future Employment Pass applications will need real diversity and local-hiring facts behind them.
nextSteps: Prepare foreign-owned-company banking documents ahead of incorporation.
Plan nationality-mix and local-hiring practices well before headcount nears 25 PMETs.
